references.title: Predicting and Interpreting Students’ Grades in Distance Higher Education through a Semi-Regression Method

extracted.Features: [Gender, New Student, Ocsi, Wrii, Logins, Pseudocode Forum views, Module forum views, Course forum views, Viewws of course news, Posts in Pseudo-Code Forum, Posts in Compiler forum, Posts in Module forum, Posts in Course Forum]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors explore Semi-Supervised Learning to perform the prediction of the exam grade.

For the Semi-supervised approach, the authors implement COREG - an adaptation of Co-training - whose quality is evaluated using a new metric called consistency.
extracted.Comments:
Paper not only focuses on modeling or SSL, but it also considers the importance of Interpretability → SHAP is adopted :)

All models are trained using the COREG framework, with selection of parameters.

Reported results show the improvement in RMSE relative to the performances  obtained from models trained exclusively using the labeled data.

annotations.text: Co-training is one form of semi-supervised classification that has historically exhibited good predictive results that has, to the author’s best knowledge not been implemented in education. Moreover, the literature lacks solutions in regression.

Authors use semi-supervised learning to predict final exam grades at the end of the academic year in undergraduate students using data from 3 different sources. Authors use a dataset provided by Hellenic Open University comparing approach against well-established ML algorithms. Results show good performance on early detection with small amounts of data.
